key_insight: A hybrid IFENN model can learn the full phase-field fracture process—initiation through propagation—while remaining transferable to unseen geometries.
engineering_relevance: This offers a lower-cost surrogate for fracture simulation that preserves physics-informed mechanics coupling and can accelerate damage prediction in design and analysis workflows.
method: FEM solves the mechanical equilibrium problem while a pretrained neural network approximates the phase-field diffusion equation.
DeepOKAN is used for fracture initiation; a CNN is used for propagation.
Training is physics-informed, done once on a benchmark geometry with very sparse, strategically sampled Gauss points.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful validation across both the training geometry and unseen geometries, with numerical accuracy maintained despite using only a few training increments and limited sampled points.
watch_item: The DeepOKAN extrapolation away from the crack tip requires artificial far-field boundary conditions, so robustness of this fix should be checked for more complex fracture scenarios.
paper_title: A hybrid IFENN solver for generalizable modeling of phase-field fracture initiation and propagation
